reviews.review_description:
Very useful if you want 3rd party web apps running in Meta quests new "seamless multitasking" my intent was/is it to run it with a couple of DAWS and Strudel (music) in a window while using VR DJing apps.  However, it's cumbersome for these reasons
- the code to connect is too long to type with alpha numeric / and capitalisation. There needs to be a copy paste or "set your pin" mechanic. This is even more crucial in a performance / live demo environ 
- the keyboard is not working even with accessibility turned on (samsung m33 5g). Could it be some new Meta HoS update breaking the input? Mic and sound are fine.
- latency compensation slider would be nice 
- pan knob for left/right for mic and audio a good frill to have.

Overall great as a solution seeing how scrcpy would need some hacking and the other one is patreon powered.

**UPDATE** the app or headset will cause a disconnect when not viewed for approximately 20 minutes or so or if your phone screen gets closed or times out. Spatial phone mega your phone to be open/ awake.. so make sure to have it set to not go to sleep for however long you'd like to saves it through your headset.

To keep it from disconnecting, an option I was shown was to use the multitasking tab feature that the meta Quest has. It's where you open your meta quest menu from your right controller (usually by pressing and holding the button with the three lines) then you can just pin that menu above your head and out of sight. It is pretty cool to have full access to your phone through this app. Anything that you can do on your phone, you can do through this app.. send text messages, make calls, watch shows and movies, play games, emails, etc.
Works great on iPhone, although I wish I could just click on the screen rather than having the separate touch surface

I currently use it for my drone but it's also possible to play games. Due to its low latency i can fly without any big problem. Contact with dev is really good, he anwsers all the questions immediately.

reviews.review_title: Do not buy, do not recommend
reviews.review_description: The app works, but barely. It cumbersome to setup and start. The window on the quest is not adjustable and is small for much of what you want to do. The “control your phone” is terrible and very misleading from the description, it doesn’t work well at all and controlling your Iphone with it is nearly impossible. The app only gets two stars because it does technically display your phone on the quest. Just with none of the functionality they make it seem like and literally zero customization of even the basic window. Not worth half this price. And there is free apps that can do everything this does.

developer_response.body:
You can resize the window on Quest by dragging the corners.

You can also control your iPhone on it. I’ve attached a video to help you set it up: https://youtu.be/Ubx-NrR_iJ4?si=R9ZjDP7ETT_HhAHr

If you’re still facing the issue mentioned above, I can offer a free 1-on-1 call to help debug and fix it via Discord (https://discord.gg/99qgmSxUZ9) or email gulzar.dev@outlook.com

It works and the only problem i could see with it is the setup time but thats because its nessessary

reviews.review_title: Awesome app that does what it's meant to
reviews.review_description:
Honest and helpful review. No bot and no sponsor or affiliate. 

First, you are looking at this app and the reviews for specific reason. Maybe you were tired of taking off your headset to check your phone to see if you received any messages or to check a message that you just heard a notification for. Maybe you're tired of turning on passthrough to look at your phone (if you have a Quest 3). Maybe you wanna watch YouTube or Disney+ or something else that's compatible (Netflix is not), while you're playing a game or on another app.

At first, because I didn't understand a couple of things. I thought the app wasn't going to meet my need. But I figured it out (user error). 

Here we go: 

Got an Android OS phone. Install, fine. App opens. Used the code that comes up in the first pop up window on the Quest that you then type into your phone, and it connects. Now...there are a couple of important things for you as the user to know and understand so you don't misunderstand like I did: 

1) Go to your Quest settings and make sure that the option for the seamless multitasking is turned on (or whatever it is called on yours). If you dont do this, it won't work.

2) Your phone screen will have to stay on. So whether you switch your timeout to a longer time frame or to always on, you have to do it. Obviously, you'd want to keep your phone plugged into a charger. If the phone screen is not on, then it won't do what it was designed to do.

3) Logging into the app. First, download the app on your phone (durr). The first time you open this VR app and the app on your phone, you get a window where you are given a code. Simply remember that code or use passthrough, and put it into the app on your phone. Then log in. Then click on the "Start Casting" button at the bottom, choose if the entire screen is shared or a specific app, and keep your phone on the entire time you want to see it. After you leave the VR app or lock your phone, you'll have to log in again, *BUT...the first pop up window for logging in will show a new code...do  NOT input that code. The app already remembers your code. So just click on the green login button and start casting.

4) Your can't use your Quest controllers to control what you are looking at. It is a viewing only app. You'd have to pair controllers with your phone to be able to do that and we know that is not possible. So if you want to navigate through your phone, use passthrough and get to the app you want to have open in VR while you play a game on VR or whatever you do in there. 

Note: Netflix doesn't project because Android doesn't allow it to be streamed due to DRM. But a ton of apps work perfectly. 

In conclusion, although it's not perfection, it is the best app for what we all came here to check. So, it's well worth it! Plus, the direct support both here and on discord is excellent, fast, and very helpful. Thanks guys!

developer_response.body:
Firstly you don't have to type in any code if you are on the same wifi network. Your phone code will automatically show up on quest.

You can increase the display timeout to prevent the phone from sleeping this way you don't have to pair your phone.

Let me know if you are still facing this issue

reviews.review_description: Please add the cursor screen on top of the actual phone screen so we won’t have to look at two screens at once. It’s counterproductive and would aid us in other apps while just looking at one screen and making sure the cursor doesn’t stay in one position. If the cursor is used in a “new state” everytime you enter the cursor screen it would help a ton. Instead of trying to fidget with it on our end you (team) could make the cursor screen restart to a new point of the screen without trying to re-adjust yourself and catch it in a good orientation. For example if I am on instagram and I swipe up my hand is outside of the cursor box. That means when I come from the right side to like the post or video I am watching the cursor will go from the top of the screen (where I left the screen) and zoom to the left. So now my original intention was to like the post but now the cursor is on the top left of my phone screen and I have to hover to the cursor screen from the top left to guide it back down. Please update this and everything else will be flawless. For anyone that doesn’t know you have to connect to WiFi,Bluetooth and then turn on “assistive touch” on your phone for this to function. TEAM please try to overlay the cursor screen on top of the phone screen. Thank you for your time.

reviews.review_description:
This app works on iphone via a modified screen-recording functionality, which actually works pretty well. There is some latency which is to be expected but it is not too bad. 

There are some issues with audio coming out of the phone as well as the headset, which is a bit of a pain, you can turn it down but it won’t go mute completely, just the lowest volume setting. Headset volume works independently, you can have it as loud as you want through the headset to drown out the quiet phone. Setup is pretty easy honestly, the code can be annoying to type in but the autoconnect for devices on the same network works well. 

One thing I wish could be worked around is that streaming apps like Netflix and Disney+ don’t work, they show a black screen, same as regular screen recording does if you try to record streaming apps. I really hope a future update can figure out some kind of work-around for this, because it would be a huge plus for this app. However, for now, “streaming” from a browser window will work just fine.
Will waste money looks nothing like in ad you will get 2 screens on ocolus one will act like touchpad very laggy as well

developer_response.body:
Hello,

Can you make sure you are connected on the same WiFi network for best performance ?
Due to apple's limitations it's only possible for touch feature to work iphone using dual screen approach.

Happy to help you over call if you are still facing. Email gulzar.dev@outlook.com

reviews.review_description:
Got this to use with iPhone since I saw some strong potential. It’s a good start, but there is still more work ahead. Some things I’ve noticed which I hope can be addressed in the future, but some of these things just could be the results of limitations with iOS.

1. Phone display and control panel are separate windows which I don’t believe is an issue with android.

2. I use steelseries headphones that can do 3.5mm and Bluetooth simultaneously. If I try to play music from my phone, neither connection will receive the audio.

3. It is a little annoying having to forget the Bluetooth connection between my phone and headset and reconnect every time I want to do a new remote session.

4. Haven’t been able to have the remote session show while playing a VR game,  I’m not sure whether to blame iOS or the VR game for that one.


Hopefully these things can be fixed for iPhone, though I can understand some of these things being limitations with trying to make this work with iOS.

developer_response.body:
Hello,

Thanks for trying out this app. I’m constantly working on improving the user experience.

1/ Unfortunately, Apple doesn’t allow external developers to control screens the way Android does. This is the only method I could get working. I’m exploring ML-based movements, but that still seems a bit far-fetched.

2/ Which app are you trying to play music from? Ideally, music should play on the Quest if you start it from your iPhone. I’ve tested with YouTube and Spotify.

3/ I know this is cumbersome. Unfortunately, this is a Quest OS issue, and I’m working with the Meta team to get it fixed.

4/ To use this alongside your game, you should enable multitasking mode.

For updates, you can follow on Discord https://discord.com/invite/99qgmSxUZ9 or email me at gulzar.dev@outlook.com.

reviews.review_title: Black screen, stream causes phone freeze
reviews.review_description:
Hi I found some problems,

+casting speed display fps
+ accessibility options enabling at the app to allow integrated vr controls causes crashes, in menu, 
+wifi connection status on 2.4 or 5ghz display need,
+ linking to cast in meta causes android app to crash and terminate on android which I have to relaunch spatial app again
+ I need app zoom in scale + and - menu button, 
+I need resolution mode with selected box to emulate resolution size drop box with auto apply, for example, 1080p, 2k, 3k, 4k in android and iPhone and meta headset spatial menu option resolution selection with video options to apply 

I need smooth portrait and landscape at the click of a button on, even if it displays on virtual portrait or landscape 

I need spatial virtual box to have a lock and unlocked box, I need fixed position display box locked, on android app and iPhone and on the meta virtual headset application next to the virtual application while running on projected(casting) virtual box, so the so it does not move around, in other words it's like running vlc in windows with the option "always on top"

I keep getting a black screen, the update claims to stop black screen but still no help 
My phone freezes during streaming button is pressed

Keep up the good work👍

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the detailed review.

A few things you mentioned are already supported in the app:

To resize windows, just drag the corner of the screen using your Quest controller.

I’ve optimized casting to be as fast as possible. If you're on the same Wi-Fi network, you don't need to enter a code — the device will automatically appear.

Most phones don’t have 4K screens, so casting at that resolution isn’t currently possible.
Changing orientation is supported in most apps and can be triggered when there's a dedicated functionality for it.

It works with iPhone but it puts a giant mouse pad to use your iPhone. A second screen to take up space while in other apps. Hopefully that pad will be able to be layered on the virtual phone screen so that it’s hidden out of site. Thanks dev this is a game changer!

Don't be fooled by the main video ad!  The video suggests that you can scroll on your phone in meta and therefor not have to touch your phone.   Basically showing that it takes input from Meta.  This is false advertising right here.  This App is worthless as it simply records your phone screen including the mic and you get horrific feedback.

reviews.review_description:
This app is brilliant in concept. I've been wanting a way to look at my iphone when I'm in VR and Meta doesn't have a way to to integrate notifications natively, so this has been a much needed tool! The only issue is that I'm not able to actually control my phone, so if I want to do something on the phone, I have to pick it up still. That's on Apple for not making the controls available to developers, but it's still worth mentioning. 

If you're looking for a way to keep track of your iPhone notifications, this is well worth the money in my opinion.

It's ok, a little laggy but that's probably my internet connection playing a part. Only really problem I have is the screeching noise when I connect everything up, I have to move my phone out of the room just to use it on the headset.

developer_response.body: The screeching noise you mentioned could be because of the mic, you can disable from your iphones mic settings or just disable the permission altogether

reviews.review_description: I like it,  is it ideal to use with iPad or iPhone?

developer_response.body:
hello

it's optimised for iPhone, but it should work with an ipad too !

reviews.review_description:
the app does everything you would expect it to for the most part you can control your phone on it and check your messages or watch youtube or whatever but you should probably know a few things first:

1) it doesnt support discord calls so if you want to use it for that then you cant (i tested with my friend and he could hear me but i couldnt hear him)

2) apps that block screen recording like netflix wont work and spotify doesnt work either (but yt music does)

3)it has a slight bit of jank to use like having to disconnect to change the app settings however once you're connected it actually works quite well even though i'm using an old phone and an outdated wifi 5 modem

overrall i'm pretty happy with it tbh and the support is excellent too even though i think it's a single dev project!

Works as advertised, think Windows Remote Desktop but for your phone. Good app and amazing capabilities given the limitations of trying to run an Android phone remotely via VR so there are some quirks, i.e. 1) when you start the app, don't press the "back" button on the first launch from the VR app or physical phone, because that shuts the app down on the phone and glitches the app in VR; 2) phone screen must remain on in order to use it in VR because if it turns off, VR will also show a black screen mirroring the phone, where you will have to wake it up again and put in your passcode/biometric/unlock pattern; 3) the phone app settings requires permissions that are not asked during install and first time launch, where they can only be found in the "Accessibility" settings on the Android phone, and such.

Having said that this is probably the only kind of app that does this and kudos to the dev for making this work. Dev is super responsive on Discord with my problems and personally helped me out when I was stuck. Well done and looking forward to better versions in the future!

Control works now in iPhone, just need to toggle Assistive touch, and works like a charm. This is so usefull when you need to charge your phone and leave the room, you can still use your phone inside VR Games too!

This app is a must if you are looking to have your phone in the VR realm.

I tried a cheaper version of this type of app and although I consider myself to be of a technical mind, the set up was awful and over my head so gave up.

Always an unknown one these sort of apps and true to form I had a challenge.  However this guys support is second to none, excellent and can't praise enough.  He was fast at replying, got straight to the issue which was a quick setting change and bingo,  up and running.

App is exactly as described and a perfect compliment to being in headset.

From my experience you should have no qualms or worries on setting up and getting it to work as the developer is first class on support.

Thank you for this app.
Works well for the better part. I will add that extra star once you make it compatible with use of VPN on phones.
This app is hella easy man a 10 years old can do it in their sleep i love this app i can keep track of all my phone notifications and texts￼ as while

reviews.review_title: Solid and exactly what I need!
reviews.review_description: Honestly I'm pretty impressed with the app so far. The stream is nice and clear assuming your WiFi is good enough, it does play through a pop up window and I hope for an app one day maybe built like how YouTube uses the roomscan to put the screen on the wall in your room. I use it for watching movies through my phone and itd exactly what I've been  looking for. Im excited to see what the developer does with it!

Recently the developer released the iphone companion app for this software and it works wonderfully very nice to be able to see my phone screen in the headset without having to take my headset off just to see if i got a text or other notification. Developer is also very helpful and answered my questions very quickly, thanks again.

Whenever I turn my phone sideways when I'm running this app the phone screen will turn completely into 1/3 of the width that it was no amount of turning will fix it and I'm wondering if the developers could look into this?

developer_response.body:
Hello 

Which device are you using can you disable high performance mode and try again?

reviews.review_title: See my heart rate tracking while I play!
reviews.review_description: Now that the move app is being discontinued I need to find a solution to see my heart rate while playing.  This is a great tool for me to see my heart rate tracking on my phone while playing.  The app works great and the developer is super responsive to any email questions.

Spatial Phone is so useful! Sometimes I get really lost in VR and lose track of time, or don't notice that I've gotten a text from my wife. As long as I keep my phone screen on and unlocked, it's really easy to pop back to it and check! It's so simple to set up. I've also been lazy about setting up YouTube on my quest and sometimes as I'm browsing, or anything else within Quest home, I will put on a video or some music in the background. I highly recommend this!
Works great any problems email and he gets back to you and sorts them out 👍

I am using a Quest 3 With an Android phone and it mirrors my phone, but I cannot.
Control it within VR Using the controllers. Is it possible to do that?

developer_response.body:
Hello 

Tp use control feature you need to give permission in the accessibility settings.

reviews.review_description:
Great app, amazing customer service, almost perfect!

 I downloaded the app a few weeks back and the issue I had appeared to freeze after a few seconds of mirroring - obviously not great...
 
However, the app designer has offered an unreal level of incredible after care service 👏 continuous support included and hour of online support too! 

It appears that my phone was disconnecting the stream (appearing to freeze) when in actual fact I was swiping a back button causing the app to stop the stream!

Can sometimes be slow response when controlling via the 'control phone from VR' function. Sometimes requiring a 'force stop' of the phone app can resolve these types of issues.

reviews.review_title: Not as good as I thought
reviews.review_description: Purchased this to use with my Drone app and didn't like the screen is really small

developer_response.body:
Hello 

You can resize the windows to increase the screen size.

If you are still facing issues I can provide help on discord https://discord.com/invite/99qgmSxUZ9 or via email at gulzar.dev@outlook.com

reviews.review_title: Exactly what I was looking for.
reviews.review_description:
What a fantastic app, it does exactly what it claims with 1st class support on Discord.

I had a few issues which was a me thing and got everything sorted in minutes with the help from the creator on Discord.  Nothing was too much trouble.  Thank you.

reviews.review_title: Good, but something weird is going on...
reviews.review_description:
Dads listen up! this is the ultimate babycall app 😅 You can literally have this up as video monitoring device while playing because the app allowed for mic passthrough. Cool stuff when the VR time is sparse and best enjoyed while the little ones sleep 😂 the app itself is good, it's kinda hit or miss if it autoupdates the join room code thing from each session. I don't think the stream is fast enough to do anything other than scroll carefully on the phone. With some delay. Other than that for me its great to keep track of time and or if I've gotten a message or whatever. It has happened before so just having the phone in the nearby while playing is super nice.

One thing that happened multiple times today that it seemed to black out my passthrough very occasionally, I had a remote desktop open and the app itself. Don't know why that happened but it was annoying so I had to close the app for my productivity session.

This is what I have been looking for years. A way to bring my phone to VR. 

It works great on portrait mode but landscape is stubborn, you will have to try several times and it will do it eventually. 

You can multitask,  I have fiver internet lowest speed,  5G router,  Samsung s24, quest 3s, V72.
My router is about 25 feet away and 2 walls in between. 

I gave it 4 stars because is not multi-touch. 

Latency is ok 👌 considering is wireless. 

Gaming is great,  I love playing my light gun 🔫 games on a big screen. 

Awesome app.

reviews.review_title: Why is paying the audio from the mic?
reviews.review_description:
The app is ok, do what needs to do, but why is recording the mic? Every time that I touch the phone I hear the sounds of the touch, or I can speak with myself if I want and hear my voice in the meta. Or if I leave the phone in other room I can hear what they are speaking. 
I guess it’s not supposed to be like this but it’s.

developer_response.body:
Hello 

You can disable mic permission from the phone setting 
Example video https://youtube.com/shorts/MSN5Lczkmhk?si=CJV4TT_09EjO0Rbn

Or deny permission when it asks during fresh install.

reviews.review_description:
I like that you can get your phone mirrored on the quest for easy access to several social apps that arent available in the quest store and dont feel like logging into via the browser, plus being able to get at your texts.  

Connecting is slightly annoying having to type in a long code that I can barely read on my phone in passthrough.  What would be nice here is a qr code that the quest app could read off of your phone with an option for the text code as a backup option.  This would make the connection experience much more pleasant as a user.  

The app can also be slightly laggy on the screen mirroring, but don't want to say for sure until I test further on a few different wifi networks.  So please don't base your decision to purchase this great app on that comment.

reviews.review_title: Keep an eye on things while in vr
reviews.review_description: The number one thing that would draw me out of a VR session would be my phone. I would hear a text or call or message and have to pause things to check. Not any more. I can connect to my phone and still play a round of Walkabout without interruption. Or if I'm using the Quest browser and need to sign into a site I can access my password manager or read the text code I need to enter to get access. So many uses. Now if they can enable making and taking calls from the headset then they get my 5th star.
I paid it with high expectations, and it doesn't cast video content, or even websites. Total waste of money...

developer_response.body:
Hello,

You should be able to cast all kinds of videos unless they are DRM-protected, like Netflix. In such cases, the system prevents screen capture.

Website capturing should work fine unless DRM-protected content is being played.

If you're still facing issues, I’d be happy to provide free support via Discord https://discord.com/invite/99qgmSxUZ9 or email gulzar.dev@outlook.com

reviews.review_description:
This works exactly like I hoped it would, it's definitely worth buying if you want an app to see and control your phone inside your headset. I actually think it works so well the developer should be charging more for it!

I bought this app on the release date (yesterday) and there was an issue with the Android app not connecting properly. I messaged the developer and they fixed it immediately and now the android app works. I can see the person before me rated this app only 1 star, which I think was hasty as now everything's fixed. If you're not sure then I'd say just buy it, as you can always get a refund within 2 weeks if you decide you don't want to use your phone in VR.

reviews.review_title: good app needs to be better for mobile g
reviews.review_description: needs better connection for mobile gaming

update 23/02/2024 : Butter Chicken is so cute , he solve hotspot problem and add HD for me app working great , dont wait for
...
...
21/02/2024 : I share internet hot spot from my s24ultra to my quest3 for outdoor work and want to use spatial phone but  screen is black in my quest 3 , sometimes connection fail

I’d hold off on buying this app if you're expecting a flawless experience. Aspect ratio switching (landscape to portrait) is buggy, and latency spikes can be frustrating. It also has Android input accessibility/security hurdles, making setup a hassle.

Right now, Windows Phone Link + Virtual Desktop is a more reliable option. This feels early access and needs time to mature. You’ll also want high-end Wi-Fi and a flagship phone for the best experience.

On the plus side, the developer has a Discord for support, so improvements are likely. But for now? It’s not quite there yet. 


---

Edit: While my review reflects my experience, I don’t want to outright discourage anyone from buying it. If you're considering this product, you’ll need a can-do attitude and an understanding that certain security mechanisms—particularly with Google and Meta—are outside the developer’s control. That said, supporting developers is key if we want products like this to improve and thrive.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for using the app and for your feedback!

- I initially planned to implement automatic resizing when switching between portrait and landscape, but Meta doesn't provide an API for developers to do that.

- For latency spikes, try lowering the quality from the Spatial app. I've considered and tested this on a wide variety of smartphones, which is why the quality option exists. In the future, there will be dedicated hardware encoder support, making it even more responsive.

- There's no way for developers to simulate controls without enabling accessibility permissions. I wish Google made this process simpler, but I understand why they don’t—it’s a very sensitive permission, and they want to prevent users from granting it accidentally.

- You don't need a flagship phone to have a good experience. By adjusting the quality settings, even a mid-range phone can provide an enjoyable experience.

developer_response.body:
Are you using over local network ?

If you are still facing issue happy to help you live on discord https://discord.com/invite/99qgmSxUZ9 or via email gulzar.dev@outlook.com

reviews.review_title: Awesome app to control your phone!
reviews.review_description: I've tried many "control your phone" apps on the quest and this one tops them all.  Well, the others don't even really work or have severe limitations.  "Spatial Phone" is small, resource friendly and works as it should.  The developer is active with his users adding, helping and troubleshooting on discord.  The price is exceptional for what it does.  No more tipping your headset up to read your phone.  I like my phone bright which doesn't always work well with passthrough mode so having the phone "inside" my headset is awesome!
